medications.indication_rationale: Softens hyperkeratotic plaques and heals fissures; the key intervention.
Restart the patient's prescribed topical psoriasis treatment to reduce plaque thickness once fissures settle.
No signs of infection; antibiotics are not indicated for an uninfected fissure with reactive erythema.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply thick petroleum jelly to the plaques and wrap loosely overnight, avoid picking the scales, don't bend the elbows sharply until softened, and use a humidifier.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Traps moisture, softens plaques and heals fissures.

patient_education_points.point: The pain and bleeding are from dry cracks in thick psoriasis plaques, not an infection — the red rim is inflammation from the skin tearing/rubbing and it fades when pressed.
Grease the patches heavily with petroleum jelly and wrap them overnight, restart your psoriasis cream once the cracks settle, don't pick the scales, and use a humidifier.

See a doctor the same day if the redness spreads up the arm (and doesn't fade under a glass), the skin becomes hot and hard, thick yellow-green pus appears, you get deep throbbing joint pain, or you develop a fever.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Restart/optimise topical psoriasis therapy; assess healing; dermatology if extensive.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Cellulitis/bursitis signs (spreading warmth/pus/fever, deep joint pain).

must_include_items: Exclude cellulitis (blanching localised rim, afebrile, no pus/warmth/streaks) and septic bursitis/joint before reassuring
Thick emollient + dressings + restart topical psoriasis therapy
errors_of_commission: Prescribing antibiotics for an uninfected fissure
ED over-triage for reactive blanching erythema
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T1/T2 case: passing requires an infection screen + emollient/dressing/topical-therapy advice + safety-net. Reflex antibiotics/ED for reactive erythema is a failure.
